2025\",\"timelineDisplayLevel\":1},\"schoolsAndDistrictsInfo\":{\"servingThisHomeSchools\":[{\"servesHome\":true,\"greatSchoolsRating\":6,\"parentRating\":4,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.3\",\"gradeRanges\":\"K-4\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Parkside Elementary School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F139284\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FParkside-Elementary-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F139284\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FParkside-Elementary-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F\",\"id\":139284,\"numberOfStudents\":506,\"fullAddress\":\"6845 SOM Center Rd, Solon, OH 44139\",\"numReviews\":3,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":16,\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048383,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"December 2025\",\"review\":\"Back when I went here, there was no dress code. Please get rid of it.\",\"reviewerRating\":3,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902209,\"maponicsId\":7314484,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048382,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"November 2020\",\"review\":\"My experience of this school was standard. The schools from my point of perspective handled bullying very well and my teachers always cared very much for my progress of learning our government. Another great thing about this school was the relations and meeting new people while also keeping up with homework and tests. Overall I think this school was a Great beginning to my life even though it had changed throughout the years.I respect this school and will never forget about all the friends and hardwork I have made.If you people disagree with me It has been some time since I went to this school so you may object to my claims as you please.\",\"reviewerRating\":4,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902209,\"maponicsId\":4758629,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":8953457,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"December 2013\",\"review\":\"Wonderful school with exceptional teachers. Top notch. I have nothing negative to say. This school is great at teaching all different types of students with various academic abilities.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"September 2024\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902209,\"maponicsId\":1421991,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":171523,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Equity\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Underserved students at this school are performing better than other students in the state, and this school is likely closing achievement gaps between different student groups.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"Using the state’s Department of Education data, the Equity Overview Rating compares this school to other schools in the state by looking at how well this school serves the needs of disadvantaged students relative to all students using state proficiency tests, student growth or academic progress data, and college readiness data. The Equity Rating was created using 2024 4-year high school graduation rate data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2023 demographic data from the National Center for Education Statistics, and using 2025 demographic data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":171521,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Test Scores\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"State test results at this school are above the state average, so students are likely performing at or above grade level.\\n\\nBecause state averages can be low, some students at this school may still not be performing at grade level.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Test Score Rating examines how students at this school performed on standardized tests compared with other schools in the state. The Test Rating was created using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":171524,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Student Growth\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"2\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Students at this school are making less academic progress from one grade to the next compared to similar students in the state.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Student Progress Rating measures whether students at this school are making academic progress over time based on student growth data provided by the Department of Education. Specifically, this rating looks at how much progress individual students have made on state assessments during the past year or more, how this performance aligns with expected progress based on a student growth model established by the state Department of Education, and how this school\\u0027s growth data compares to other schools in the state. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City School District\",\"address\":\"33800 Inwood Drive\",\"city\":\"Solon\",\"stateCode\":\"OH\",\"zip\":\"44139\",\"latitude\":41.377995,\"longitude\":-81.43895,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fsolonschools.org\u002F\",\"greatschoolDistrictOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002Fsolon-city-school-district\u002F\"},\"greatschoolParentReviewsUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F#Reviews\",\"elementary\":true,\"middle\":false,\"high\":false,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"December 2025\",\"hasMultipleCatchmentAreas\":false},{\"servesHome\":true,\"greatSchoolsRating\":9,\"parentRating\":2,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.3\",\"gradeRanges\":\"5-7\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Orchard Middle School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F131213\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FOrchard-Middle-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F131213\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FOrchard-Middle-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\",\"id\":131213,\"numberOfStudents\":733,\"fullAddress\":\"6800 SOM Center Rd, Solon, OH 44139\",\"numReviews\":3,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":15,\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048399,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2026\",\"review\":\"The principal violated my child’s fourth amendment rights by searching his backpack claiming that she was looking for a lighter that someone told her he had which he didn’t . She then looked through his papers and claimed to “clean his backpack” upon which she discovered that he had post it notes rolled up like a you know what. I was appalled. She never apologized to my son either when I asked her too. When they put you on some list it’s like anything that your child does, then they don’t even talk to them they just report it to the principal and will give them a detention. It’s like a prison.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902212,\"maponicsId\":7346517,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048398,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"November 2025\",\"review\":\"This school is excessively punitive! Excessively! Operates more like a prison! Do yourself a favor and transfer your child before they get to this place!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902212,\"maponicsId\":7282391,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":5961257,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"August 2013\",\"review\":\"I agree with one of the previous posters about the math program not being tweaked for gifted students. My son was in an excel program in Cuyahoga Falls and when we told the school that they kind of brushed us off, but I assumed because Solon held their schools to a higher standard and most schools excel programs was the norm for Solon. But now that he is a 7th grader at Solon Middle school they saw his test scores and NOW they are advancing him to 8th grade algebra. But with that being said...Solon schools have so much more to offer and the communication between the school, the staff and the parents is awesome! We get emails about upcoming events and if there was a disruption at the school (ie...electricity went out one time and they sent an email stating the problem and another when it was back up). I LOVE SOLON!!!\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"September 2024\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902212,\"maponicsId\":1373235,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":171613,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Test Scores\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"State test results at this school are above the state average, so students are likely performing at or above grade level.\\n\\nBecause state averages can be low, some students at this school may still not be performing at grade level.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Test Score Rating examines how students at this school performed on standardized tests compared with other schools in the state. The Test Rating was created using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":171615,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Equity\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Underserved students at this school are performing better than other students in the state, and this school is likely closing achievement gaps between different student groups.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"Using the state’s Department of Education data, the Equity Overview Rating compares this school to other schools in the state by looking at how well this school serves the needs of disadvantaged students relative to all students using state proficiency tests, student growth or academic progress data, and college readiness data. The Equity Rating was created using 2024 4-year high school graduation rate data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2023 demographic data from the National Center for Education Statistics, and using 2025 demographic data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":171612,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Student Growth\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"8\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Students at this school are making more academic progress from one grade to the next compared to similar students in the state.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Student Progress Rating measures whether students at this school are making academic progress over time based on student growth data provided by the Department of Education. Specifically, this rating looks at how much progress individual students have made on state assessments during the past year or more, how this performance aligns with expected progress based on a student growth model established by the state Department of Education, and how this school\\u0027s growth data compares to other schools in the state. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City School District\",\"address\":\"33800 Inwood Drive\",\"city\":\"Solon\",\"stateCode\":\"OH\",\"zip\":\"44139\",\"latitude\":41.377995,\"longitude\":-81.43895,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fsolonschools.org\u002F\",\"greatschoolDistrictOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002Fsolon-city-school-district\u002F\"},\"greatschoolParentReviewsUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F#Reviews\",\"elementary\":true,\"middle\":true,\"high\":false,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"December 2025\",\"hasMultipleCatchmentAreas\":false},{\"servesHome\":false,\"greatSchoolsRating\":9,\"parentRating\":2,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.4\",\"gradeRanges\":\"7-8\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Solon Middle School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F99059\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-Middle-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F99059\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-Middle-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\",\"id\":99059,\"numberOfStudents\":769,\"fullAddress\":\"6835 SOM Center Rd, Solon, OH 44139\",\"isNearby\":true,\"numReviews\":9,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":13,\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048391,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2026\",\"review\":\"My family had the worst experience with this school last school year. The assistant principal is clueless about middle schoolers and unfortunately, the principal takes her side. When the staff lied on my preteen about a hand gesture that they were apparently clueless about, the coach lied and said that my son was making a violent gesture even when my son denied it. They waited several days to follow up without even notifying us as his parents. Thankfully we were at the event where the coach claimed to imply that my son made a violent hand gesture and we saw no such thing. The assistant principal still suspended my son and the principal defended the assistant principal. We disputed the issue and had requested footage of the event which proved my son’s innocence. They have a new assistant and head principal this year. At the time, the lying coaches, assistant and head principal made no apology to my son for what they caused. The administration is absolutely horrible for children. Only thing they care about are state tests which they made sure my son was in school to take while they waited several days to decide to suspend our child. They violated his rights so I am sure that is why they also took away the suspension. Watch this school very carefully with your children.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":7346499,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048390,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2023\",\"review\":\"I have to echo the comments of the other reviewer on 3.25.22 below. However I am a parent not a student. My son had the same experience they didn\\u0027t say the N word instead they called his name and made money sounds. The principal Campbell explained it away as a \\\"game\\\" ..which my son wasn\\u0027t playing btw.. no action. Then to add insult to injury when my son\\u0027s TOURETTES a documented ada protected disability flares and coprolalia showed up by way of expletives and vulgarity suddenly there are suspensions flying all over the place. They seem to have policy for sick kids but not for HATE CRIMES! SUPERINTENDENT is passive pretends to care shows minimal movement and school board silent everyone sits on their hands. Not one person even followed up to see if my son was ok! My son\\u0027s teaching staff is awesome I hate he will lose their support change is another ignitor of anxiety\u002Ftourettes but his mental health is my main priority!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":5761176,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048389,\"reviewedBy\":\"Student\",\"datePosted\":\"April 2022\",\"review\":\"Solon Middle School is excellent. I have gone to Roxbury Elementary School, Orchard Middle School, and next year I will be attending Solon High School and I cannot be more prepared.Learning: SMS excels at their education system. They are one of the best in the state, and have teachers who teach very well. I have never felt unprepared for an assessment as the teachers always review. Homework is not to bad, it could be much worse. For some classes, there is virtually no homework unless you don\\u0027t finish in class.Teaching: All the teachers I have come across are wonderful. They are great at their jobs and always review before an assessment so their students are well prepared. They are also very kind and makes the class entertaining sometimes. However, they do know when to be serious and focus on teaching.Social-Emotional Support: SMS has a counselor who will always help you, but the teachers are there for you too. Even if you aren\\u0027t their student, you can go to them for just about anything. They also do not tolerate jokes about emotional subjects and I have seen people being commended for doing so. Special Education:I have seen my fair share of kids who have special disabilities and all of them have guides who help and instruct them throughout their day. I have even worked with a kid with a disability and everything went fine as the guide helped and made sure my partner was focused.Safety: SMS makes sure all their students are safe and routinely have drills and make sure the students know what to do in case of emergency. They also have a system for people who don\\u0027t do homework so that it can help them do so. They do have punishments, but they are only inflicted on people who have done wrong.Family Engagement: SMS is very good at communicating with family. They send out emails to inform parents and even students can be notified about things like tests. The teachers also usually respond with a day\\u0027s notice. Overall, SMS is a great school to go to. I have never felt any hard emotions going to this school, but I guess the experience is different for everyone. However, this school is amazing when it comes to education and the teachers are all great. The school does their best to accommodate others and makes sure that everyone at their school is safe. I love SMS and I will be sad to leave it when I go to high school.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":5385442,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048388,\"reviewedBy\":\"Student\",\"datePosted\":\"March 2022\",\"review\":\"Going to this school has been one of the worst experiences of my life. The people who watch over us at lunch scream at us and act as if we are 4 year olds, the entire staff does. Staff is stuck in the past and continues to silence voices of children and distance themselves from self thought. Teachers act as if students who need more help in some subjects are just stupid. With the \\u0027No Tolerance Policy\\u0027 if someone hits you or calls you something you just have to take it, you can\\u0027t do anything. Someone got suspended for giving someone advil. The rules are incredibly dumb and the principle, Mr. Campbell, doesn\\u0027t care about any bigotry faced by students. A white girl called a black boy the n-word for beating her is basketball and Campbell told him to just get over it. If you want a safe school where your kids will actually feel safe and heard do not send your kids here.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":5355386,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048387,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"April 2017\",\"review\":\"I was student at Solon Middle School and graduated from the Solon School System in 1999. My parents moved my family to Solon because of their educational standards and reputation and I feel that I received an excellent education as a result. So reading the negative reviews is quite shocking because that was not my experience at all. In middle school my teachers were very engaged and creative in their lesson plans and classroom teaching. Certainly there were subjects that I liked more than others and so would feel bored at times, not because of lack of effort from the teachers, but more so my interests\u002Flack there of. However, there were certainly times that my teachers made subjects that I found boring interesting as well. For those students who excelled in a subject, particularly math, those students were provided the opportunity to take classes at the high school to ensure that they are being academically challenged (and in high school at post secondary institutions). Clearly, its been years since I was a student at SMS so changes inevitably have occurred. However, I currently work as an academic adviser at an Ohio university and the students that I see coming from Solon have good things to say about the school system and feel prepared for college level coursework.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":2566563,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048386,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"February 2017\",\"review\":\"My son has had an amazing experience at Solon Middle School. His teachers are engaged and responsive.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":2463484,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048385,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"February 2017\",\"review\":\"This school focused on only the standar tests and its ranking, not the individual education at all. My son was an A student, but was bored and frustrated at this school. I contacted the principal about this issue many times and proposed my idea to solve it. The principal was very arbitrary and tried to use Pizza to erase the rooting problem! The new yong assistant principal cooperated the principal very well. He punished my son with detention without noticement and my permission!! It is an awefull school!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":2458892,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":172157,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Equity\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Underserved students at this school are performing better than other students in the state, and this school is likely closing achievement gaps between different student groups.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"Using the state’s Department of Education data, the Equity Overview Rating compares this school to other schools in the state by looking at how well this school serves the needs of disadvantaged students relative to all students using state proficiency tests, student growth or academic progress data, and college readiness data. The Equity Rating was created using 2024 4-year high school graduation rate data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2023 demographic data from the National Center for Education Statistics, and using 2025 demographic data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":172159,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Test Scores\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"State test results at this school are above the state average, so students are likely performing at or above grade level.\\n\\nBecause state averages can be low, some students at this school may still not be performing at grade level.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Test Score Rating examines how students at this school performed on standardized tests compared with other schools in the state. The Test Rating was created using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":172160,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Student Growth\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"8\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Students at this school are making more academic progress from one grade to the next compared to similar students in the state.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Student Progress Rating measures whether students at this school are making academic progress over time based on student growth data provided by the Department of Education. Specifically, this rating looks at how much progress individual students have made on state assessments during the past year or more, how this performance aligns with expected progress based on a student growth model established by the state Department of Education, and how this school\\u0027s growth data compares to other schools in the state. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City School District\",\"address\":\"33800 Inwood Drive\",\"city\":\"Solon\",\"stateCode\":\"OH\",\"zip\":\"44139\",\"latitude\":41.377995,\"longitude\":-81.43895,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fsolonschools.org\u002F\",\"greatschoolDistrictOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002Fsolon-city-school-district\u002F\"},\"greatschoolParentReviewsUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F#Reviews\",\"elementary\":false,\"middle\":true,\"high\":false,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"January 2026\",\"hasMultipleCatchmentAreas\":false},{\"servesHome\":true,\"greatSchoolsRating\":10,\"parentRating\":2,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.5\",\"gradeRanges\":\"9-12\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Solon High School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F74611\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-High-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F74611\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-High-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\",\"id\":74611,\"numberOfStudents\":1450,\"fullAddress\":\"33600 Inwood Dr, Solon, OH 44139\",\"numReviews\":10,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":16,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fwww.solonschools.org\u002Fshs\",\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048397,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"December 2025\",\"review\":\"I didn\\u0027t go to this high school. And I\\u0027m glad I didn\\u0027t. But the reason I was no stranger to this school was because I used to be on swim team. And on swim meets, there was a mean and rude lady who accused me of playing in the water in the diving pool when I was not! She even threatened to ban me from the diving pool! So I lap swim again and she disallowed me from swimming in the diving pool! She is not the boss of me! I want that lady fired for the way she treated me!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":7296514,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048396,\"reviewedBy\":\"Student\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2024\",\"review\":\"This school is one of the best schools in ohio, and it lives up to that name.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":6256586,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048395,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"June 2022\",\"review\":\"The school purposefully hid diversity and inclusion materials being taught to the students from the parents. Parents contacted the high school principal and the superintendent asking to see these materials after they heard concerning comments from their children; both claimed that the materials were best to be seen AFTER the presentation in order to have \\\"organic conversations\\\" with the students. They claimed parents could always opt their children out of the lessons, yet they refused to share with the parents when the lesson would be taking place; it was only communicated after the lesson was presented to the students. A definition of controversial terms were also never sent to or posted on their website; these were emailed directly to the student only. The principal also sent an email to parents recommending controversial reading and stated they needed to be mindful of their privilege. Lastly, the superintendent claimed he reviewed and approved all of the material, so one has to wonder about the HS principal and the superintendent\\u0027s judgement. When the school board was contacted, they just sent the parents back to the superintendent, who then sends you back to the principal, who then sends you back to the superintendent. Requested feedback after the lessons from both teachers and students were that the lessons were highly inappropriate to be in a public school setting; the materials were political in nature, were not based on fact, and was put together by the PTA. Several students felt like it was saying that only certain ethnic groups were capable of racism. In addition, the PTA insisted the materials needed to be kept a secret from parents unless you paid to be a PTA member, yet PTA parents were also refused the material. It wasn\\u0027t until the community came together to share their frustration and disappointment, which included showing samples of the material, at the school board meeting to finally end the secrecy.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":5467013,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048394,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"April 2021\",\"review\":\"school is not the greatest they over give work and expect for you to do it the next day also not very supportive\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":4937450,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048393,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"August 2016\",\"review\":\"Solon Schools are public schools. If you think that you are getting \\\"quality\\\" education at solon schools and the food that solon schools serve in their cafeterias is \\\"healthy\\\", then you should consider yourself as ignorant and brainwashed. Read the details.\",\"reviewerRating\":3,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":2244955,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048392,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"June 2015\",\"review\":\"I read the reviews on this site 6 years back. Now my child is out of school and its time to provide my take. School district\\u003dCorporation. Superintendent\\u003dCEO. Principals\\u003dDeputy Sheriffs. Assistant Principals\\u003dAssistant Sheriffs, Teachers\\u003dEmployees, Students\\u003dClients. School Board members give an illusion that residents have a say in the functioning of the school. They don\\u0027t. Curriculum made in Columbus. Solon schools are good, not too good, not too bad. Enjoy the entertainment. Just be glad that its tax free, so far. GLTA.\",\"reviewerRating\":3,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":1631762,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":4949200,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"September 2013\",\"review\":\"Needs to get rid of some rotten teachers that are old, tired, fumpy, and stuck.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"September 2024\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":1384658,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":172372,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"College Readiness\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"This school is above the state average in key measures of college and career readiness, such as graduation rates, college entrance exams, and advanced courses.\\n\\nEven at schools with strong college and career readiness, there may be students who are not getting the opportunities they need to succeed.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The College Readiness Rating uses this high school\\u0027s graduation rates, college entrance exam participation and performance, or AP, IB, or Dual Enrollment participation to determine how well schools are preparing students for success in college and beyond. The College Readiness Rating was created using the following data from the 2021 Civil Rights Data Collection: percentage of students enrolled in IB, AP or Dual Enrollment classes in grades 9-12, using 2024 4-year high school graduation rate data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 ACT participation data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 SAT percent participation data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 SAT percent college ready data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 ACT percent college ready data from Ohio Department of Education, and using 2025 demographic data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":172369,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Test Scores\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"9\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"State test results at this school are above the state average, so students are likely performing at or above grade level.\\n\\nBecause state averages can be low, some students at this school may still not be performing at grade level.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Test Score Rating examines how students at this school performed on standardized tests compared with other schools in the state. The Test Rating was created using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":172370,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Student Growth\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Students at this school are making more academic progress from one grade to the next compared to similar students in the state.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Student Progress Rating measures whether students at this school are making academic progress over time based on student growth data provided by the Department of Education. Specifically, this rating looks at how much progress individual students have made on state assessments during the past year or more, how this performance aligns with expected progress based on a student growth model established by the state Department of Education, and how this school\\u0027s growth data compares to other schools in the state. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":172371,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Equity\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"9\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Underserved students at this school are performing better than other students in the state, and this school is likely closing achievement gaps between different student groups.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"Using the state’s Department of Education data, the Equity Overview Rating compares this school to other schools in the state by looking at how well this school serves the needs of disadvantaged students relative to all students using state proficiency tests, student growth or academic progress data, and college readiness data. The Equity Rating was created using 2024 4-year high school graduation rate data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2023 demographic data from the National Center for Education Statistics, and using 2025 demographic data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City SOM Center Rd, Solon, OH 44139\",\"numReviews\":3,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":16,\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048383,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"December 2025\",\"review\":\"Back when I went here, there was no dress code. Please get rid of it.\",\"reviewerRating\":3,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902209,\"maponicsId\":7314484,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048382,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"November 2020\",\"review\":\"My experience of this school was standard. The schools from my point of perspective handled bullying very well and my teachers always cared very much for my progress of learning our government. Another great thing about this school was the relations and meeting new people while also keeping up with homework and tests. Overall I think this school was a Great beginning to my life even though it had changed throughout the years.I respect this school and will never forget about all the friends and hardwork I have made.If you people disagree with me It has been some time since I went to this school so you may object to my claims as you please.\",\"reviewerRating\":4,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902209,\"maponicsId\":4758629,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":8953457,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"December 2013\",\"review\":\"Wonderful school with exceptional teachers. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City School District\",\"address\":\"33800 Inwood Drive\",\"city\":\"Solon\",\"stateCode\":\"OH\",\"zip\":\"44139\",\"latitude\":41.377995,\"longitude\":-81.43895,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fsolonschools.org\u002F\",\"greatschoolDistrictOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002Fsolon-city-school-district\u002F\"},\"greatschoolParentReviewsUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2209-Parkside-Elementary-School\u002F#Reviews\",\"elementary\":true,\"middle\":false,\"high\":false,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"December 2025\",\"hasMultipleCatchmentAreas\":false},{\"servesHome\":true,\"greatSchoolsRating\":9,\"parentRating\":2,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.3\",\"gradeRanges\":\"5-7\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Orchard Middle School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F131213\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FOrchard-Middle-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F131213\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FOrchard-Middle-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\",\"id\":131213,\"numberOfStudents\":733,\"fullAddress\":\"6800 SOM Center Rd, Solon, OH 44139\",\"numReviews\":3,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":15,\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048399,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2026\",\"review\":\"The principal violated my child’s fourth amendment rights by searching his backpack claiming that she was looking for a lighter that someone told her he had which he didn’t . She then looked through his papers and claimed to “clean his backpack” upon which she discovered that he had post it notes rolled up like a you know what. I was appalled. She never apologized to my son either when I asked her too. When they put you on some list it’s like anything that your child does, then they don’t even talk to them they just report it to the principal and will give them a detention. It’s like a prison.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902212,\"maponicsId\":7346517,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048398,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"November 2025\",\"review\":\"This school is excessively punitive! Excessively! Operates more like a prison! Do yourself a favor and transfer your child before they get to this place!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902212,\"maponicsId\":7282391,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":5961257,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"August 2013\",\"review\":\"I agree with one of the previous posters about the math program not being tweaked for gifted students. My son was in an excel program in Cuyahoga Falls and when we told the school that they kind of brushed us off, but I assumed because Solon held their schools to a higher standard and most schools excel programs was the norm for Solon. But now that he is a 7th grader at Solon Middle school they saw his test scores and NOW they are advancing him to 8th grade algebra. But with that being said...Solon schools have so much more to offer and the communication between the school, the staff and the parents is awesome! We get emails about upcoming events and if there was a disruption at the school (ie...electricity went out one time and they sent an email stating the problem and another when it was back up). I LOVE SOLON!!!\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"September 2024\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902212,\"maponicsId\":1373235,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":171613,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Test Scores\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"State test results at this school are above the state average, so students are likely performing at or above grade level.\\n\\nBecause state averages can be low, some students at this school may still not be performing at grade level.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Test Score Rating examines how students at this school performed on standardized tests compared with other schools in the state. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City School District\",\"address\":\"33800 Inwood Drive\",\"city\":\"Solon\",\"stateCode\":\"OH\",\"zip\":\"44139\",\"latitude\":41.377995,\"longitude\":-81.43895,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fsolonschools.org\u002F\",\"greatschoolDistrictOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002Fsolon-city-school-district\u002F\"},\"greatschoolParentReviewsUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2212-Orchard-Middle-School\u002F#Reviews\",\"elementary\":true,\"middle\":true,\"high\":false,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"December 2025\",\"hasMultipleCatchmentAreas\":false},{\"servesHome\":true,\"greatSchoolsRating\":10,\"parentRating\":2,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.5\",\"gradeRanges\":\"9-12\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Solon High School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F74611\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-High-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F74611\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-High-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\",\"id\":74611,\"numberOfStudents\":1450,\"fullAddress\":\"33600 Inwood Dr, Solon, OH 44139\",\"numReviews\":10,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":16,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fwww.solonschools.org\u002Fshs\",\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048397,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"December 2025\",\"review\":\"I didn\\u0027t go to this high school. And I\\u0027m glad I didn\\u0027t. But the reason I was no stranger to this school was because I used to be on swim team. And on swim meets, there was a mean and rude lady who accused me of playing in the water in the diving pool when I was not! She even threatened to ban me from the diving pool! So I lap swim again and she disallowed me from swimming in the diving pool! She is not the boss of me! I want that lady fired for the way she treated me!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":7296514,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048396,\"reviewedBy\":\"Student\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2024\",\"review\":\"This school is one of the best schools in ohio, and it lives up to that name.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":6256586,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048395,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"June 2022\",\"review\":\"The school purposefully hid diversity and inclusion materials being taught to the students from the parents. Parents contacted the high school principal and the superintendent asking to see these materials after they heard concerning comments from their children; both claimed that the materials were best to be seen AFTER the presentation in order to have \\\"organic conversations\\\" with the students. They claimed parents could always opt their children out of the lessons, yet they refused to share with the parents when the lesson would be taking place; it was only communicated after the lesson was presented to the students. A definition of controversial terms were also never sent to or posted on their website; these were emailed directly to the student only. The principal also sent an email to parents recommending controversial reading and stated they needed to be mindful of their privilege. Lastly, the superintendent claimed he reviewed and approved all of the material, so one has to wonder about the HS principal and the superintendent\\u0027s judgement. When the school board was contacted, they just sent the parents back to the superintendent, who then sends you back to the principal, who then sends you back to the superintendent. Requested feedback after the lessons from both teachers and students were that the lessons were highly inappropriate to be in a public school setting; the materials were political in nature, were not based on fact, and was put together by the PTA. Several students felt like it was saying that only certain ethnic groups were capable of racism. In addition, the PTA insisted the materials needed to be kept a secret from parents unless you paid to be a PTA member, yet PTA parents were also refused the material. It wasn\\u0027t until the community came together to share their frustration and disappointment, which included showing samples of the material, at the school board meeting to finally end the secrecy.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":5467013,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048394,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"April 2021\",\"review\":\"school is not the greatest they over give work and expect for you to do it the next day also not very supportive\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":4937450,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048393,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"August 2016\",\"review\":\"Solon Schools are public schools. If you think that you are getting \\\"quality\\\" education at solon schools and the food that solon schools serve in their cafeterias is \\\"healthy\\\", then you should consider yourself as ignorant and brainwashed. Read the details.\",\"reviewerRating\":3,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":2244955,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048392,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"June 2015\",\"review\":\"I read the reviews on this site 6 years back. Now my child is out of school and its time to provide my take. School district\\u003dCorporation. Superintendent\\u003dCEO. Principals\\u003dDeputy Sheriffs. Assistant Principals\\u003dAssistant Sheriffs, Teachers\\u003dEmployees, Students\\u003dClients. School Board members give an illusion that residents have a say in the functioning of the school. They don\\u0027t. Curriculum made in Columbus. Solon schools are good, not too good, not too bad. Enjoy the entertainment. Just be glad that its tax free, so far. GLTA.\",\"reviewerRating\":3,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":1631762,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":4949200,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"September 2013\",\"review\":\"Needs to get rid of some rotten teachers that are old, tired, fumpy, and stuck.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"September 2024\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902214,\"maponicsId\":1384658,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":172372,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"College Readiness\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"This school is above the state average in key measures of college and career readiness, such as graduation rates, college entrance exams, and advanced courses.\\n\\nEven at schools with strong college and career readiness, there may be students who are not getting the opportunities they need to succeed.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The College Readiness Rating uses this high school\\u0027s graduation rates, college entrance exam participation and performance, or AP, IB, or Dual Enrollment participation to determine how well schools are preparing students for success in college and beyond. Specifically, this rating looks at how much progress individual students have made on state assessments during the past year or more, how this performance aligns with expected progress based on a student growth model established by the state Department of Education, and how this school\\u0027s growth data compares to other schools in the state. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":172371,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Equity\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"9\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Underserved students at this school are performing better than other students in the state, and this school is likely closing achievement gaps between different student groups.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"Using the state’s Department of Education data, the Equity Overview Rating compares this school to other schools in the state by looking at how well this school serves the needs of disadvantaged students relative to all students using state proficiency tests, student growth or academic progress data, and college readiness data. The Equity Rating was created using 2024 4-year high school graduation rate data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2023 demographic data from the National Center for Education Statistics, and using 2025 demographic data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City School District\",\"address\":\"33800 Inwood Drive\",\"city\":\"Solon\",\"stateCode\":\"OH\",\"zip\":\"44139\",\"latitude\":41.377995,\"longitude\":-81.43895,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fsolonschools.org\u002F\",\"greatschoolDistrictOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002Fsolon-city-school-district\u002F\"},\"greatschoolParentReviewsUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2214-Solon-High-School\u002F#Reviews\",\"elementary\":false,\"middle\":false,\"high\":true,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"December 2025\",\"hasMultipleCatchmentAreas\":false},{\"servesHome\":false,\"greatSchoolsRating\":10,\"parentRating\":5,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.3\",\"gradeRanges\":\"K-4\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Grace L Roxbury Elementary School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F51729\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FGrace-L-Roxbury-Elementary-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F51729\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FGrace-L-Roxbury-Elementary-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2213-Grace-L-Roxbury-Elementary-School\u002F\",\"id\":51729,\"numberOfStudents\":479,\"fullAddress\":\"6795 Solon Blvd, Solon, OH 44139\",\"isNearby\":true,\"numReviews\":2,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":16,\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":5961253,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"August 2013\",\"review\":\"Moved from Akron to Solon (THANK GOD) and Solon schools have so much more to offer and the communication between the school, the staff and the parents is awesome! We get emails about upcoming events and if there was a disruption at the school (ie...electricity went out one time and they sent an email stating the problem and another when it was back up). I LOVE SOLON!!!\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"September 2024\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902213,\"maponicsId\":1373227,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2213-Grace-L-Roxbury-Elementary-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":14737246,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"March 2013\",\"review\":\"We found the School Excellent, and my daughter she is doing great. Thank you Roxbury School.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"September 2024\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902213,\"maponicsId\":1304247,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2213-Grace-L-Roxbury-Elementary-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":173762,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Student Growth\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Students at this school are making more academic progress from one grade to the next compared to similar students in the state.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Student Progress Rating measures whether students at this school are making academic progress over time based on student growth data provided by the Department of Education. Specifically, this rating looks at how much progress individual students have made on state assessments during the past year or more, how this performance aligns with expected progress based on a student growth model established by the state Department of Education, and how this school\\u0027s growth data compares to other schools in the state. The Growth Rating was created using 2025 Student growth data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":173761,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Test Scores\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"State test results at this school are above the state average, so students are likely performing at or above grade level.\\n\\nBecause state averages can be low, some students at this school may still not be performing at grade level.\\n\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"The Test Score Rating examines how students at this school performed on standardized tests compared with other schools in the state. The Test Rating was created using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education.\"},{\"schoolId\":173763,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Equity\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Underserved students at this school are performing better than other students in the state, and this school is likely closing achievement gaps between different student groups.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"Using the state’s Department of Education data, the Equity Overview Rating compares this school to other schools in the state by looking at how well this school serves the needs of disadvantaged students relative to all students using state proficiency tests, student growth or academic progress data, and college readiness data. The Equity Rating was created using 2024 4-year high school graduation rate data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2025 Ohio State Test data from Ohio Department of Education, using 2023 demographic data from the National Center for Education Statistics, and using 2025 demographic data from Ohio Department of Education.\"}],\"schoolDistrict\":{\"id\":8108,\"districtName\":\"Solon City School District\",\"address\":\"33800 Inwood Drive\",\"city\":\"Solon\",\"stateCode\":\"OH\",\"zip\":\"44139\",\"latitude\":41.377995,\"longitude\":-81.43895,\"websiteUrl\":\"http:\u002F\u002Fsolonschools.org\u002F\",\"greatschoolDistrictOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002Fsolon-city-school-district\u002F\"},\"greatschoolParentReviewsUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Fsolon\u002F2213-Grace-L-Roxbury-Elementary-School\u002F#Reviews\",\"elementary\":true,\"middle\":false,\"high\":false,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"December 2025\",\"hasMultipleCatchmentAreas\":false},{\"servesHome\":false,\"greatSchoolsRating\":9,\"parentRating\":2,\"distanceInMiles\":\"1.4\",\"gradeRanges\":\"7-8\",\"institutionType\":\"Public\",\"name\":\"Solon Middle School\",\"schoolUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F99059\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-Middle-School\",\"searchUrl\":\"\u002Fschool\u002F99059\u002FOH\u002FSolon\u002FSolon-Middle-School\",\"greatSchoolOverviewUrl\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\",\"id\":99059,\"numberOfStudents\":769,\"fullAddress\":\"6835 SOM Center Rd, Solon, OH 44139\",\"isNearby\":true,\"numReviews\":9,\"studentToTeacherRatio\":13,\"schoolReviews\":[{\"schoolId\":46048391,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2026\",\"review\":\"My family had the worst experience with this school last school year. The assistant principal is clueless about middle schoolers and unfortunately, the principal takes her side. When the staff lied on my preteen about a hand gesture that they were apparently clueless about, the coach lied and said that my son was making a violent gesture even when my son denied it. They waited several days to follow up without even notifying us as his parents. Thankfully we were at the event where the coach claimed to imply that my son made a violent hand gesture and we saw no such thing. The assistant principal still suspended my son and the principal defended the assistant principal. We disputed the issue and had requested footage of the event which proved my son’s innocence. They have a new assistant and head principal this year. At the time, the lying coaches, assistant and head principal made no apology to my son for what they caused. The administration is absolutely horrible for children. Only thing they care about are state tests which they made sure my son was in school to take while they waited several days to decide to suspend our child. They violated his rights so I am sure that is why they also took away the suspension. Watch this school very carefully with your children.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":7346499,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048390,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"January 2023\",\"review\":\"I have to echo the comments of the other reviewer on 3.25.22 below. However I am a parent not a student. My son had the same experience they didn\\u0027t say the N word instead they called his name and made money sounds. The principal Campbell explained it away as a \\\"game\\\" ..which my son wasn\\u0027t playing btw.. no action. Then to add insult to injury when my son\\u0027s TOURETTES a documented ada protected disability flares and coprolalia showed up by way of expletives and vulgarity suddenly there are suspensions flying all over the place. They seem to have policy for sick kids but not for HATE CRIMES! SUPERINTENDENT is passive pretends to care shows minimal movement and school board silent everyone sits on their hands. Not one person even followed up to see if my son was ok! My son\\u0027s teaching staff is awesome I hate he will lose their support change is another ignitor of anxiety\u002Ftourettes but his mental health is my main priority!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":5761176,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048389,\"reviewedBy\":\"Student\",\"datePosted\":\"April 2022\",\"review\":\"Solon Middle School is excellent. I have gone to Roxbury Elementary School, Orchard Middle School, and next year I will be attending Solon High School and I cannot be more prepared.Learning: SMS excels at their education system. They are one of the best in the state, and have teachers who teach very well. I have never felt unprepared for an assessment as the teachers always review. Homework is not to bad, it could be much worse. For some classes, there is virtually no homework unless you don\\u0027t finish in class.Teaching: All the teachers I have come across are wonderful. They are great at their jobs and always review before an assessment so their students are well prepared. They are also very kind and makes the class entertaining sometimes. However, they do know when to be serious and focus on teaching.Social-Emotional Support: SMS has a counselor who will always help you, but the teachers are there for you too. Even if you aren\\u0027t their student, you can go to them for just about anything. They also do not tolerate jokes about emotional subjects and I have seen people being commended for doing so. Special Education:I have seen my fair share of kids who have special disabilities and all of them have guides who help and instruct them throughout their day. I have even worked with a kid with a disability and everything went fine as the guide helped and made sure my partner was focused.Safety: SMS makes sure all their students are safe and routinely have drills and make sure the students know what to do in case of emergency. They also have a system for people who don\\u0027t do homework so that it can help them do so. They do have punishments, but they are only inflicted on people who have done wrong.Family Engagement: SMS is very good at communicating with family. They send out emails to inform parents and even students can be notified about things like tests. The teachers also usually respond with a day\\u0027s notice. Overall, SMS is a great school to go to. I have never felt any hard emotions going to this school, but I guess the experience is different for everyone. However, this school is amazing when it comes to education and the teachers are all great. The school does their best to accommodate others and makes sure that everyone at their school is safe. I love SMS and I will be sad to leave it when I go to high school.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":5385442,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048388,\"reviewedBy\":\"Student\",\"datePosted\":\"March 2022\",\"review\":\"Going to this school has been one of the worst experiences of my life. The people who watch over us at lunch scream at us and act as if we are 4 year olds, the entire staff does. Staff is stuck in the past and continues to silence voices of children and distance themselves from self thought. Teachers act as if students who need more help in some subjects are just stupid. With the \\u0027No Tolerance Policy\\u0027 if someone hits you or calls you something you just have to take it, you can\\u0027t do anything. Someone got suspended for giving someone advil. The rules are incredibly dumb and the principle, Mr. Campbell, doesn\\u0027t care about any bigotry faced by students. A white girl called a black boy the n-word for beating her is basketball and Campbell told him to just get over it. If you want a safe school where your kids will actually feel safe and heard do not send your kids here.\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":5355386,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048387,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"April 2017\",\"review\":\"I was student at Solon Middle School and graduated from the Solon School System in 1999. My parents moved my family to Solon because of their educational standards and reputation and I feel that I received an excellent education as a result. So reading the negative reviews is quite shocking because that was not my experience at all. In middle school my teachers were very engaged and creative in their lesson plans and classroom teaching. Certainly there were subjects that I liked more than others and so would feel bored at times, not because of lack of effort from the teachers, but more so my interests\u002Flack there of. However, there were certainly times that my teachers made subjects that I found boring interesting as well. For those students who excelled in a subject, particularly math, those students were provided the opportunity to take classes at the high school to ensure that they are being academically challenged (and in high school at post secondary institutions). Clearly, its been years since I was a student at SMS so changes inevitably have occurred. However, I currently work as an academic adviser at an Ohio university and the students that I see coming from Solon have good things to say about the school system and feel prepared for college level coursework.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":2566563,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048386,\"reviewedBy\":\"Parent\",\"datePosted\":\"February 2017\",\"review\":\"My son has had an amazing experience at Solon Middle School. His teachers are engaged and responsive.\",\"reviewerRating\":5,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":2463484,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"},{\"schoolId\":46048385,\"reviewedBy\":\"Other\",\"datePosted\":\"February 2017\",\"review\":\"This school focused on only the standar tests and its ranking, not the individual education at all. My son was an A student, but was bored and frustrated at this school. I contacted the principal about this issue many times and proposed my idea to solve it. The principal was very arbitrary and tried to use Pizza to erase the rooting problem! The new yong assistant principal cooperated the principal very well. He punished my son with detention without noticement and my permission!! It is an awefull school!\",\"reviewerRating\":1,\"lastUpdatedDate\":\"February 2026\",\"institutionGreatschoolId\":3902208,\"maponicsId\":2458892,\"url\":\"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.greatschools.org\u002Fohio\u002Ftwinsburg\u002F2208-Solon-Middle-School\u002F\"}],\"schoolGranularRatings\":[{\"schoolId\":172157,\"dataSourceId\":633,\"schoolGranularRatingType\":\"Equity\",\"schoolGranularRatingValue\":\"10\",\"schoolGranularRatingCopyText\":\"Underserved students at this school are performing better than other students in the state, and this school is likely closing achievement gaps between different student groups.\",\"schoolGranularRatingsDescription\":\"Using the state’s Department of Education data, the Equity Overview Rating compares this school to other schools in the state by looking at how well this school serves the needs of disadvantaged students relative to all students using state proficiency tests, student growth or academic progress data, and college readiness data.
